meaning: adj.不同种类的 异类的
examples: Geologists have long known that the Earth's mantle is heterogeneous, but its spatial arrangement remains unresolved—is the mantle essentially layered or irregu larly heterogeneous?
Glycans can be heterogeneous as well, consisting of many different glycoforms attached to one site.
In fact, our Articles of Confederation might be an even better model, given how heterogeneous Europe is.
Rather heterogeneous and equivocal stratigraphic definitions were found during the revision of existing literature.
